mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding real economic activity and rising corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon. The mechanism is always active and does not require a threshold trigger.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change against forward 20-day returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 55% of months with positive INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, it signals severe labor market slack and depressed consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal activates only when UNRATE crosses above the threshold.
failure_condition: More than 40% of triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns, or the average return in triggered regimes is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in the labor market—falling nonfarm payrolls combined with rising unemployment—amplifies negative sentiment and consumption risk, producing a stronger negative signal for forward equity returns than either series alone. The interaction is required to capture the signal.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS decline & UNRATE increase) fails to produce a significant negative mean forward return (rank IC < 0.03 or t-test p > 0.05), or the signal is explained by either series individually.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strengthening consumer demand and residential investment, which should lead to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as the recovery matures.
failure_condition: In periods where both proxies rise month-over-month, forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the Spearman rank correlation between the composite demand signal and returns is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation, as measured by rising CPI, erodes real purchasing power and increases the likelihood of future monetary tightening, which should depress equity valuations over short weekly horizons. The signal is always active.
failure_condition: The weekly change in CPIAUCSL has Rank IC above -0.02 (i.e., not sufficiently negative) over any 1-year rolling window, or forward 5-day returns during CPI increases are not significantly lower than during declines.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1,400 thousand units, it signals a weakening housing sector that depresses household wealth and construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is triggered only when the series crosses below the threshold.
failure_condition: More than 50% of triggered weeks produce positive 5-day returns, or the average return in triggered weeks is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of extremely low federal funds rates and rising CPI creates negative real interest rates, which historically has been associated with financial repression and currency debasement fears, leading to negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon. The interaction between monetary accommodation and inflation is essential.
failure_condition: The interaction of low FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL fails to produce a negative mean forward 60-day return (rank IC > -0.02 or t-test p > 0.10), or the signal is dominated by either series individually.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of high unemployment (UNRATE above 7.0%), a strong recovery in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS increasing) signals the start of labor market healing and should lead to positive forward returns as the economy emerges from the trough. This regime-conditional signal is directionally positive only in the 'high unemployment recovery' regime.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE > 7.0, rising PAYEMS does not lead to statistically significant positive forward 20-day returns (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's rank IC over such regimes is below 0.03.
